title: End of module: Mechanics and Energy

questions.text: A force of $40\,\text{N}$ acts at $60^\circ$ above the horizontal. Find the magnitude of its horizontal component.
An object has initial velocity $u = 6\,\text{m s}^{-1}$ and accelerates uniformly at $a = 1.5\,\text{m s}^{-2}$ for $t = 8\,\text{s}$. Find its final velocity $v$.
An object starts from rest ($u = 0$) and accelerates uniformly at $a = 4\,\text{m s}^{-2}$. How far does it travel in $t = 5\,\text{s}$?
A ball is launched at $u = 16\,\text{m s}^{-1}$ at $50^\circ$ above the horizontal, from and landing at ground level ($g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$). Find its time of flight.
For the same launch as the previous question ($u = 16\,\text{m s}^{-1}$, $\theta = 50^\circ$, $g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$), find the horizontal range.
Newton's first law states that an object…
choices: always moves in a straight line at constant speed
remains at rest, or moving at constant velocity, unless acted on by a resultant external force
accelerates in direct proportion to the force applied to it
exerts an equal and opposite force on any object it touches
questions.explanation: Newton's first law is the law of inertia: without a resultant external force, velocity (including zero velocity) does not change.

questions.text: According to Newton's third law, the two forces of an action-reaction pair…
choices: act on the same object and cancel out
are equal in magnitude, opposite in direction, and act on two different objects
are only equal in magnitude if the two objects have equal mass
act in the same direction on the same object
questions.explanation: Third-law pairs are always equal in magnitude and opposite in direction, but act on two different objects — so they never cancel each other out.

questions.text: The equation $F_{\text{net}} = \dfrac{\Delta p}{\Delta t}$ (which reduces to $F = ma$ for constant mass) is a statement of which of Newton's laws? Name it.
questions.explanation: This is Newton's second law: the resultant force equals the rate of change of momentum, which simplifies to $F=ma$ when the mass is constant.

questions.text: A $3\,\text{kg}$ trolley moving at $4\,\text{m s}^{-1}$ collides with a stationary $2\,\text{kg}$ trolley and they stick together. Find their common velocity afterwards.
questions.explanation: By conservation of momentum: $(3)(4) + (2)(0) = (3+2)v$, so $12 = 5v$, giving $v = 2.4\,\text{m s}^{-1}$.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about collisions are correct?
choices: Momentum is conserved in both elastic and inelastic collisions, provided no external resultant force acts
Kinetic energy is conserved in an elastic collision
Kinetic energy is always conserved in any collision, elastic or inelastic
In a perfectly inelastic collision, the colliding bodies stick together and move off with a common velocity
questions.explanation: Momentum is conserved in every collision (statement 1). Kinetic energy is conserved only in an elastic collision (statement 2 true, statement 3 false — inelastic collisions lose kinetic energy to heat, sound and deformation). A perfectly inelastic collision is defined by the bodies sticking together with one common final velocity (statement 4).

questions.text: A rope pulls a sled $10\,\text{m}$ across level snow. The rope is held at $30^\circ$ to the direction of travel and has tension $25\,\text{N}$. Find the work done by the rope.
Find the kinetic energy of a $2\,\text{kg}$ object moving at $6\,\text{m s}^{-1}$.
Find the gravitational potential energy of a $1.5\,\text{kg}$ mass raised $4\,\text{m}$ ($g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$).
A crane lifts a $250\,\text{kg}$ load a height of $8\,\text{m}$ in $20\,\text{s}$ at constant velocity ($g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$). Find the power output.
